# Helia

Helia
-----

[![Helia logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ipfs/helia/main/assets/helia.png)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia "Helia")

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/helia.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/helia)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/helia/main.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/actions/workflows/main.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

[Helia](https://github.com/ipfs/helia)
 is a lean, modular, and modern TypeScript implementation of IPFS for the prolific JS and browser environments.

See the [Manifesto](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/Manifesto)
, the [FAQ](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/FAQ)
, and the [State of IPFS in JS blog post from October 2022](https://blog.ipfs.tech/state-of-ipfs-in-js/)
 for more info.

[🌟 Usage](#md:🌟-usage)

=========================

A quick overview of how to get different types of data in and out of your Helia node.

[🪢 Strings](#md:🪢-strings)

-----------------------------

You can use the [@helia/strings](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/strings)
 module to easily add and get strings from your Helia node:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { strings } from '@helia/strings'const helia = await createHelia()const s = strings(helia)const myImmutableAddress = await s.add('hello world')console.log(await s.get(myImmutableAddress))// hello world
    

[🌃 JSON](#md:🌃-json)

-----------------------

The [@helia/json](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/json)
 module lets you add or get plain JS objects:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { json } from '@helia/json'const helia = await createHelia()const j = json(helia)const myImmutableAddress = await j.add({ hello: 'world' })console.log(await j.get(myImmutableAddress))// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🌠 DAG-JSON](#md:🌠-dag-json)

-------------------------------

The [@helia/dag-json](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/dag-json)
 allows you to store references to linked objects as [CIDs](https://docs.ipfs.tech/concepts/content-addressing)
:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagJson } from '@helia/dag-json'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagJson(helia)const object1 = { hello: 'world' }const myImmutableAddress1 = await d.add(object1)const object2 = { link: myImmutableAddress1 }const myImmutableAddress2 = await d.add(object2)const retrievedObject = await d.get(myImmutableAddress2)console.log(retrievedObject)// { link: CID(baguqeerasor...) }console.log(await d.get(retrievedObject.link))// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🌌 DAG-CBOR](#md:🌌-dag-cbor)

-------------------------------

[@helia/dag-cbor](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/dag-cbor)
 works in a similar way to `@helia/dag-json` but stores objects using [Concise Binary Object Representation](https://cbor.io/)
:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagCbor(helia)const object1 = { hello: 'world' }const myImmutableAddress1 = await d.add(object1)const object2 = { link: myImmutableAddress1 }const myImmutableAddress2 = await d.add(object2)const retrievedObject = await d.get(myImmutableAddress2)console.log(retrievedObject)// { link: CID(baguqeerasor...) }console.log(await d.get(retrievedObject.link))// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🔒 Custom Hasher](#md:🔒-custom-hasher)

-----------------------------------------

A [hasher](https://github.com/multiformats/js-multiformats?tab=readme-ov-file#multihash-hashers)
 is used to determine the immutable address of the content (aka the [**CID**](https://github.com/multiformats/cid?tab=readme-ov-file#what-is-it)
) being imported into helia. The default hasher used by the methods above is [sha2-256 multihash](https://github.com/multiformats/js-multiformats?tab=readme-ov-file#multihash-hashers-1)
, but others can be provided with [AddOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/helia/interfaces/_helia_dag_cbor.AddOptions.html)
. This is useful for applications that require hashers with specific properties; so in most cases keeping the default is recommended.

> **Changing the hasher will cause a different CID to be returned for the same content! In other words: the same content imported with different hashers is treated like unique content with a unique address.**

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'import { sha512 } from 'multiformats/hashes/sha2'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagCbor(helia)const object1 = { hello: 'world' }const cidWithSHA256 = await d.add(object1)const cidWithSHA512 = await d.add(object1, {  hasher: sha512})/** The same objects with different CIDs are treated as different objects */console.log(cidWithSHA256)// CID(bafyreidykglsfhoixmivffc5uwhcgshx4j465xwqntbmu43nb2dzqwfvae)console.log(cidWithSHA512)// CID(bafyrgqhai26anf3i7pips7q22coa4sz2fr4gk4q4sqdtymvvjyginfzaqewveaeqdh524nsktaq43j65v22xxrybrtertmcfxufdam3da3hbk)const retrievedObject1 = await d.get(cidWithSHA256)const retrievedObject2 = await d.get(cidWithSHA512)console.log(retrievedObject1)// { hello: 'world' }console.log(retrievedObject2)// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🐾 Next steps](#md:🐾-next-steps)

===================================

Check out the [helia-examples](https://github.com/ipfs-examples/helia-examples)
 repo for how to do mostly anything with your Helia node.

[🏃‍♀️ Getting Started](#md:🏃♀️-getting-started)

==================================================

Check out the [Helia examples repo](https://github.com/ipfs-examples/helia-examples#examples)
, which covers a wide variety of use cases. If you feel something has been missed, follow the [contribution guide](https://github.com/ipfs-examples/helia-examples#contributing)
 and create a PR to the examples repo.

[📗 Project Docs](#md:📗-project-docs)

=======================================

*   See the [project wiki](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki)
    .

[📒 API Docs](#md:📒-api-docs)

===============================

*   [https://ipfs.github.io/helia](https://ipfs.github.io/helia)
    

[📐 System diagram](#md:📐-system-diagram)

===========================================

    graph TD;    User["User or application"]-->IPNS["@helia/ipns"];    User-->UnixFS["@helia/unixfs"];    User-->Libp2p;    User-->Datastore;    User-->Blockstore;    UnixFS-->Blockstore;    IPNS-->Datastore;    subgraph helia [Helia]      Datastore      Blockstore-->BlockBrokers;      BlockBrokers-->Bitswap;      BlockBrokers-->TrustlessGateways;      Libp2p-->DHT;      Libp2p-->PubSub;      Libp2p-->IPNI;      Libp2p-->Reframe;    end    Blockstore-->BlockStorage["File system/IDB/S3/etc"];    Datastore-->DataStorage["Level/S3/IDB/etc"];    Bitswap-->Network;    TrustlessGateways-->Gateway1;    TrustlessGateways-->GatewayN;    DHT-->Network;    PubSub-->Network;    IPNI-->Network;    Reframe-->Network;
    

[🏭 Code Structure](#md:🏭-code-structure)

===========================================

Helia embraces a modular approach and encourages users to bring their own implementations of various APIs to suit their needs.

The basic Helia API is defined in:

*   [`/packages/interface`](./packages/interface)
     The Helia API

The API is implemented by:

*   [`/packages/helia`](./packages/helia)
     An peer to peer implementation that uses [bitswap](https://docs.ipfs.tech/concepts/bitswap/)
    , [libp2p](https://www.npmjs.com/package/libp2p)
     and [HTTP gateways](https://docs.ipfs.tech/reference/http/gateway/)
     as fallback
*   [`/packages/http`](./packages/http)
     A lightweight implementation that uses [HTTP gateways](https://docs.ipfs.tech/reference/http/gateway/)
     exclusively

Helia also ships a number of supplemental libraries and tools that can be combined with Helia API implementations to accomplish tasks in distributed and trustless ways.

These libraries are not intended to be the "one true implementation" of any given API, but are made available for users to include depending on the need of their particular application:

*   [./packages/car](./packages/car)
     The `@helia/car` module
*   [./packages/dag-cbor](./packages/dag-cbor)
     The `@helia/dag-cbor` module
*   [./packages/dag-json](./packages/dag-json)
     The `@helia/dag-json` module
*   [./packages/ipns](./packages/ipns)
     The `@helia/ipns` module
*   [./packages/json](./packages/json)
     The `@helia/json` module
*   [./packages/mfs](./packages/mfs)
     The `@helia/mfs` module
*   [./packages/strings](./packages/strings)
     The `@helia/strings` module
*   [./packages/unixfs](./packages/unixfs)
     The `@helia/unixfs` module

An interop suite ensures everything is compatible:

*   [`/packages/interop`](./packages/interop)
     Interop tests for Helia

[Other modules](#md:other-modules)

-----------------------------------

There are several other modules available outside this repo:

*   [`@helia/verified-fetch`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-verified-fetch)
     A [fetch](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fetch_API)
    \-like API for retrieving trustless, verified content from the distributed web
*   [`@helia/delegated-routing-v1-http-api`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-delegated-routing-v1-http-api)
     An implementation of the [Delegated Routing v1 HTTP API](https://specs.ipfs.tech/routing/http-routing-v1/)
     including a server and a client
*   [Helia WNFS](https://github.com/shovelers/helia-wnfs)
     a [WNFS](https://guide.fission.codes/developers/webnative/file-system-wnfs)
     implementation built on top of Helia
*   [`@helia/remote-pinning`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-remote-pinning)
     A Helia client for communicating with [IPFS Pinning Services](https://ipfs.github.io/pinning-services-api-spec/)
    
*   [`@helia/http-gateway`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-http-gateway)
     An implementation of the [IPFS HTTP Gateway API](https://docs.ipfs.tech/concepts/ipfs-gateway/#gateway-types)
     built with Helia

[📣 Project status](#md:📣-project-status)

===========================================

Helia v1 shipped in 202303 (see [releases](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/releases)
), and development keeps on trucking as we work on initiatives in the [roadmap](#md:roadmap)
 and make performance improvements and bug fixes along the way.

[Release Process](#md:release-process)

---------------------------------------

For information about our release process, please see our [release process wiki](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/Development-and-Release-Process#how-helia-is-released)
.

[🛣️ Roadmap](#md:🛣️-roadmap)

===============================

Please find and comment on [the Roadmap here](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues/5)
.

[👫 Get involved](#md:👫-get-involved)

=======================================

*   Watch our Helia Demo Day presentations [here](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLuhRWgmPaHtQAnt8INOe5-kV9TLVaUJ9v)
    
*   We are sharing about the progress at periodic [Helia Demos](https://lu.ma/helia)
    . This is a good place to find out the latest and learn of ways to get involved. We'd love to see you there!
*   Pick up one of the [issues](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues)
    .
*   Come chat in Filecoin Slack #ip-js. (Yes, we should bridge this to other chat environments. Please comment [here](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues/33)
     if you'd like this.)

[🤲 Contribute](#md:🤲-contribute)

===================================

Contributions welcome! Please check out [the issues](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues)
.

Also see our [contributing document](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING_JS.md)
 for more information on how we work, and about contributing in general.

Please be aware that all interactions related to this repo are subject to the IPFS [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/code-of-conduct.md)
.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

[![](https://cdn.rawgit.com/jbenet/contribute-ipfs-gif/master/img/contribute.gif)](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

# Helia

Helia
-----

[![Helia logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ipfs/helia/main/assets/helia.png)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia "Helia")

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/helia.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/helia)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/helia/main.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/actions/workflows/main.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

[Helia](https://github.com/ipfs/helia)
 is a lean, modular, and modern TypeScript implementation of IPFS for the prolific JS and browser environments.

See the [Manifesto](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/Manifesto)
, the [FAQ](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/FAQ)
, and the [State of IPFS in JS blog post from October 2022](https://blog.ipfs.tech/state-of-ipfs-in-js/)
 for more info.

[🌟 Usage](#md:🌟-usage)

=========================

A quick overview of how to get different types of data in and out of your Helia node.

[🪢 Strings](#md:🪢-strings)

-----------------------------

You can use the [@helia/strings](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/strings)
 module to easily add and get strings from your Helia node:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { strings } from '@helia/strings'const helia = await createHelia()const s = strings(helia)const myImmutableAddress = await s.add('hello world')console.log(await s.get(myImmutableAddress))// hello world
    

[🌃 JSON](#md:🌃-json)

-----------------------

The [@helia/json](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/json)
 module lets you add or get plain JS objects:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { json } from '@helia/json'const helia = await createHelia()const j = json(helia)const myImmutableAddress = await j.add({ hello: 'world' })console.log(await j.get(myImmutableAddress))// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🌠 DAG-JSON](#md:🌠-dag-json)

-------------------------------

The [@helia/dag-json](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/dag-json)
 allows you to store references to linked objects as [CIDs](https://docs.ipfs.tech/concepts/content-addressing)
:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagJson } from '@helia/dag-json'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagJson(helia)const object1 = { hello: 'world' }const myImmutableAddress1 = await d.add(object1)const object2 = { link: myImmutableAddress1 }const myImmutableAddress2 = await d.add(object2)const retrievedObject = await d.get(myImmutableAddress2)console.log(retrievedObject)// { link: CID(baguqeerasor...) }console.log(await d.get(retrievedObject.link))// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🌌 DAG-CBOR](#md:🌌-dag-cbor)

-------------------------------

[@helia/dag-cbor](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/dag-cbor)
 works in a similar way to `@helia/dag-json` but stores objects using [Concise Binary Object Representation](https://cbor.io/)
:

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagCbor(helia)const object1 = { hello: 'world' }const myImmutableAddress1 = await d.add(object1)const object2 = { link: myImmutableAddress1 }const myImmutableAddress2 = await d.add(object2)const retrievedObject = await d.get(myImmutableAddress2)console.log(retrievedObject)// { link: CID(baguqeerasor...) }console.log(await d.get(retrievedObject.link))// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🔒 Custom Hasher](#md:🔒-custom-hasher)

-----------------------------------------

A [hasher](https://github.com/multiformats/js-multiformats?tab=readme-ov-file#multihash-hashers)
 is used to determine the immutable address of the content (aka the [**CID**](https://github.com/multiformats/cid?tab=readme-ov-file#what-is-it)
) being imported into helia. The default hasher used by the methods above is [sha2-256 multihash](https://github.com/multiformats/js-multiformats?tab=readme-ov-file#multihash-hashers-1)
, but others can be provided with [AddOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/helia/interfaces/_helia_dag_cbor.AddOptions.html)
. This is useful for applications that require hashers with specific properties; so in most cases keeping the default is recommended.

> **Changing the hasher will cause a different CID to be returned for the same content! In other words: the same content imported with different hashers is treated like unique content with a unique address.**

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'import { sha512 } from 'multiformats/hashes/sha2'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagCbor(helia)const object1 = { hello: 'world' }const cidWithSHA256 = await d.add(object1)const cidWithSHA512 = await d.add(object1, {  hasher: sha512})/** The same objects with different CIDs are treated as different objects */console.log(cidWithSHA256)// CID(bafyreidykglsfhoixmivffc5uwhcgshx4j465xwqntbmu43nb2dzqwfvae)console.log(cidWithSHA512)// CID(bafyrgqhai26anf3i7pips7q22coa4sz2fr4gk4q4sqdtymvvjyginfzaqewveaeqdh524nsktaq43j65v22xxrybrtertmcfxufdam3da3hbk)const retrievedObject1 = await d.get(cidWithSHA256)const retrievedObject2 = await d.get(cidWithSHA512)console.log(retrievedObject1)// { hello: 'world' }console.log(retrievedObject2)// { hello: 'world' }
    

[🐾 Next steps](#md:🐾-next-steps)

===================================

Check out the [helia-examples](https://github.com/ipfs-examples/helia-examples)
 repo for how to do mostly anything with your Helia node.

[🏃‍♀️ Getting Started](#md:🏃♀️-getting-started)

==================================================

Check out the [Helia examples repo](https://github.com/ipfs-examples/helia-examples#examples)
, which covers a wide variety of use cases. If you feel something has been missed, follow the [contribution guide](https://github.com/ipfs-examples/helia-examples#contributing)
 and create a PR to the examples repo.

[📗 Project Docs](#md:📗-project-docs)

=======================================

*   See the [project wiki](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki)
    .

[📒 API Docs](#md:📒-api-docs)

===============================

*   [https://ipfs.github.io/helia](https://ipfs.github.io/helia)
    

[📐 System diagram](#md:📐-system-diagram)

===========================================

    graph TD;    User["User or application"]-->IPNS["@helia/ipns"];    User-->UnixFS["@helia/unixfs"];    User-->Libp2p;    User-->Datastore;    User-->Blockstore;    UnixFS-->Blockstore;    IPNS-->Datastore;    subgraph helia [Helia]      Datastore      Blockstore-->BlockBrokers;      BlockBrokers-->Bitswap;      BlockBrokers-->TrustlessGateways;      Libp2p-->DHT;      Libp2p-->PubSub;      Libp2p-->IPNI;      Libp2p-->Reframe;    end    Blockstore-->BlockStorage["File system/IDB/S3/etc"];    Datastore-->DataStorage["Level/S3/IDB/etc"];    Bitswap-->Network;    TrustlessGateways-->Gateway1;    TrustlessGateways-->GatewayN;    DHT-->Network;    PubSub-->Network;    IPNI-->Network;    Reframe-->Network;
    

[🏭 Code Structure](#md:🏭-code-structure)

===========================================

Helia embraces a modular approach and encourages users to bring their own implementations of various APIs to suit their needs.

The basic Helia API is defined in:

*   [`/packages/interface`](./packages/interface)
     The Helia API

The API is implemented by:

*   [`/packages/helia`](./packages/helia)
     An peer to peer implementation that uses [bitswap](https://docs.ipfs.tech/concepts/bitswap/)
    , [libp2p](https://www.npmjs.com/package/libp2p)
     and [HTTP gateways](https://docs.ipfs.tech/reference/http/gateway/)
     as fallback
*   [`/packages/http`](./packages/http)
     A lightweight implementation that uses [HTTP gateways](https://docs.ipfs.tech/reference/http/gateway/)
     exclusively

Helia also ships a number of supplemental libraries and tools that can be combined with Helia API implementations to accomplish tasks in distributed and trustless ways.

These libraries are not intended to be the "one true implementation" of any given API, but are made available for users to include depending on the need of their particular application:

*   [./packages/car](./packages/car)
     The `@helia/car` module
*   [./packages/dag-cbor](./packages/dag-cbor)
     The `@helia/dag-cbor` module
*   [./packages/dag-json](./packages/dag-json)
     The `@helia/dag-json` module
*   [./packages/ipns](./packages/ipns)
     The `@helia/ipns` module
*   [./packages/json](./packages/json)
     The `@helia/json` module
*   [./packages/mfs](./packages/mfs)
     The `@helia/mfs` module
*   [./packages/strings](./packages/strings)
     The `@helia/strings` module
*   [./packages/unixfs](./packages/unixfs)
     The `@helia/unixfs` module

An interop suite ensures everything is compatible:

*   [`/packages/interop`](./packages/interop)
     Interop tests for Helia

[Other modules](#md:other-modules)

-----------------------------------

There are several other modules available outside this repo:

*   [`@helia/verified-fetch`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-verified-fetch)
     A [fetch](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fetch_API)
    \-like API for retrieving trustless, verified content from the distributed web
*   [`@helia/delegated-routing-v1-http-api`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-delegated-routing-v1-http-api)
     An implementation of the [Delegated Routing v1 HTTP API](https://specs.ipfs.tech/routing/http-routing-v1/)
     including a server and a client
*   [Helia WNFS](https://github.com/shovelers/helia-wnfs)
     a [WNFS](https://guide.fission.codes/developers/webnative/file-system-wnfs)
     implementation built on top of Helia
*   [`@helia/remote-pinning`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-remote-pinning)
     A Helia client for communicating with [IPFS Pinning Services](https://ipfs.github.io/pinning-services-api-spec/)
    
*   [`@helia/http-gateway`](https://github.com/ipfs/helia-http-gateway)
     An implementation of the [IPFS HTTP Gateway API](https://docs.ipfs.tech/concepts/ipfs-gateway/#gateway-types)
     built with Helia

[📣 Project status](#md:📣-project-status)

===========================================

Helia v1 shipped in 202303 (see [releases](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/releases)
), and development keeps on trucking as we work on initiatives in the [roadmap](#md:roadmap)
 and make performance improvements and bug fixes along the way.

[Release Process](#md:release-process)

---------------------------------------

For information about our release process, please see our [release process wiki](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/Development-and-Release-Process#how-helia-is-released)
.

[🛣️ Roadmap](#md:🛣️-roadmap)

===============================

Please find and comment on [the Roadmap here](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues/5)
.

[👫 Get involved](#md:👫-get-involved)

=======================================

*   Watch our Helia Demo Day presentations [here](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLuhRWgmPaHtQAnt8INOe5-kV9TLVaUJ9v)
    
*   We are sharing about the progress at periodic [Helia Demos](https://lu.ma/helia)
    . This is a good place to find out the latest and learn of ways to get involved. We'd love to see you there!
*   Pick up one of the [issues](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues)
    .
*   Come chat in Filecoin Slack #ip-js. (Yes, we should bridge this to other chat environments. Please comment [here](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues/33)
     if you'd like this.)

[🤲 Contribute](#md:🤲-contribute)

===================================

Contributions welcome! Please check out [the issues](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues)
.

Also see our [contributing document](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING_JS.md)
 for more information on how we work, and about contributing in general.

Please be aware that all interactions related to this repo are subject to the IPFS [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/code-of-conduct.md)
.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

[![](https://cdn.rawgit.com/jbenet/contribute-ipfs-gif/master/img/contribute.gif)](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

[🛍️ Notable Consumers/Users](#md:🛍️-notable-consumersusers)

==============================================================

*   See [Projects using Helia](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/Projects-using-Helia)
    .

[🌞 Branding](#md:🌞-branding)

===============================

*   See [Branding](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/wiki/Branding)
    .

[🪪 License](#md:🪪-license)

=============================

Licensed under either of

*   Apache 2.0, ([LICENSE-APACHE](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/blob/main/LICENSE-APACHE)
     / [http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
    )
*   MIT ([LICENSE-MIT](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/blob/main/LICENSE-MIT)
     / [http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT](http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
    )

;

# @helia/dag-cbor | Helia

*   [Helia](../index.html)
    
*   [@helia/dag-cbor](_helia_dag_cbor.html)
    

Module @helia/dag-cbor
======================

`@helia/dag-cbor` makes working with DAG-JSON [Helia](https://github.com/ipfs/helia)
 simple & straightforward.

See the [DAGCBOR](../interfaces/_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html)
 interface for all available operations.

#### Example

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'import { CID } from 'multiformats/cid'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagCbor(helia)const cid = await d.add({  hello: 'world'})const obj = await d.get(cid)console.info(obj)// { hello: 'world' }
    

[![Helia logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ipfs/helia/main/assets/helia.png)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia "Helia")

[@helia/dag-cbor](#md:heliadag-cbor)

=====================================

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/helia.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/helia)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/helia/main.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/actions/workflows/main.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

> Add/get IPLD blocks containing dag-cbor with your Helia node

[About](#md:about)

===================

`@helia/dag-cbor` makes working with DAG-JSON Helia simple & straightforward.

See the DAGCBOR interface for all available operations.

[Example](#md:example)

-----------------------

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'import { CID } from 'multiformats/cid'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagCbor(helia)const cid = await d.add({  hello: 'world'})const obj = await d.get(cid)console.info(obj)// { hello: 'world' }
    

[Install](#md:install)

=======================

    $ npm i @helia/dag-cbor
    

[Browser `<script>` tag](#md:browser-ltscriptgt-tag)

-----------------------------------------------------

Loading this module through a script tag will make its exports available as `HeliaDagCbor` in the global namespace.

    <script src="https://unpkg.com/@helia/dag-cbor/dist/index.min.js"></script>
    

[API Docs](#md:api-docs)

=========================

*   [https://ipfs.github.io/helia/modules/\_helia\_dag\_cbor.html](https://ipfs.github.io/helia/modules/_helia_dag_cbor.html)
    

[License](#md:license)

=======================

Licensed under either of

*   Apache 2.0, ([LICENSE-APACHE](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/blob/main/packages/dag-cbor/LICENSE-APACHE)
     / [http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
    )
*   MIT ([LICENSE-MIT](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/blob/main/packages/dag-cbor/LICENSE-MIT)
     / [http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT](http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
    )

[Contribute](#md:contribute)

=============================

Contributions welcome! Please check out [the issues](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues)
.

Also see our [contributing document](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING_JS.md)
 for more information on how we work, and about contributing in general.

# Page not found · GitHub Pages

404
===

**File not found**

The site configured at this address does not contain the requested file.

If this is your site, make sure that the filename case matches the URL as well as any file permissions.  
For root URLs (like `http://example.com/`) you must provide an `index.html` file.

[Read the full documentation](https://help.github.com/pages/)
 for more information about using **GitHub Pages**.

# helia | Helia

*   [Helia](../index.html)
    
*   [helia](helia.html)
    

Module helia
============

Exports a `createHelia` function that returns an object that implements the [Helia](../interfaces/helia.Helia.html)
 API.

Pass it to other modules like [@helia/unixfs](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@helia/unixfs)
 to make files available on the distributed web.

#### Example

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { unixfs } from '@helia/unixfs'import { CID } from 'multiformats/cid'const helia = await createHelia()const fs = unixfs(helia)fs.cat(CID.parse('bafyFoo'))
    

[![Helia logo](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ipfs/helia/main/assets/helia.png)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia "Helia")

[helia](#md:helia)

===================

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/helia.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/helia)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/helia/main.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/actions/workflows/main.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

> An implementation of IPFS in JavaScript

[About](#md:about)

===================

Exports a `createHelia` function that returns an object that implements the Helia API.

Pass it to other modules like @helia/unixfs to make files available on the distributed web.

[Example](#md:example)

-----------------------

    import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { unixfs } from '@helia/unixfs'import { CID } from 'multiformats/cid'const helia = await createHelia()const fs = unixfs(helia)fs.cat(CID.parse('bafyFoo'))
    

[Install](#md:install)

=======================

    $ npm i helia
    

[Browser `<script>` tag](#md:browser-ltscriptgt-tag)

-----------------------------------------------------

Loading this module through a script tag will make its exports available as `Helia` in the global namespace.

    <script src="https://unpkg.com/helia/dist/index.min.js"></script>
    

[API Docs](#md:api-docs)

=========================

*   [https://ipfs.github.io/helia/modules/helia.html](https://ipfs.github.io/helia/modules/helia.html)
    

[License](#md:license)

=======================

Licensed under either of

*   Apache 2.0, ([LICENSE-APACHE](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/blob/main/packages/helia/LICENSE-APACHE)
     / [http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
    )
*   MIT ([LICENSE-MIT](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/blob/main/packages/helia/LICENSE-MIT)
     / [http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT](http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
    )

[Contribute](#md:contribute)

=============================

Contributions welcome! Please check out [the issues](https://github.com/ipfs/helia/issues)
.

Also see our [contributing document](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING_JS.md)
 for more information on how we work, and about contributing in general.

Please be aware that all interactions related to this repo are subject to the IPFS [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/code-of-conduct.md)
.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

[![](https://cdn.rawgit.com/jbenet/contribute-ipfs-gif/master/img/contribute.gif)](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

# DAGCBOR | Helia

*   [Helia](../index.html)
    
*   [@helia/dag-cbor](../modules/_helia_dag_cbor.html)
    
*   [DAGCBOR](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html)
    

Interface DAGCBOR
=================

The JSON interface provides a simple and intuitive way to add/get objects with your Helia node and is a great place to start learning about IPFS.

interface DAGCBOR {  
    [add](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#add.add-1)
(str, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
<unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
\>\>;  
    [get](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#get.get-1)
<[T](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#get.get-1.T)
\>(cid, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[T](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#get.get-1.T)
\>;  
}

##### Index

### Methods

[add](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#add)
 [get](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#get)

Methods
-------

### add[](#add)

*   add(str, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#add.add-1)
    
*   Add an object to your Helia node and get a CID that refers to the block the object has been stored as.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   str: unknown
    *   `Optional` options: [Partial](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#partialtype)
        <[AddOptions](_helia_dag_cbor.AddOptions.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'const d = dagCbor(helia)const cid = await d.add({ hello: 'world' })console.info(cid)// CID(bafyreidykglsfhoixmivffc5uwhcgshx4j465xwqntbmu43nb2dzqwfvae)
        
    

### get[](#get)

*   get<[T](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#get.get-1.T)
    \>(cid, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[T](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#get.get-1.T)
    \>[](#get.get-1)
    
*   Get an object from your Helia node, either previously added to it or to another node on the network.
    
    #### Type Parameters
    
    *   T
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   cid: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [Partial](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#partialtype)
        <[GetOptions](_helia_dag_cbor.GetOptions.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[T](_helia_dag_cbor.DAGCBOR.html#get.get-1.T)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        import { createHelia } from 'helia'import { dagCbor } from '@helia/dag-cbor'import { CID } from 'multiformats/cid'const helia = await createHelia()const d = dagCbor(helia)const cid = CID.parse('bafyreidykglsfhoixmivffc5uwhcgshx4j465xwqntbmu43nb2dzqwfvae')const obj = await d.get(cid)console.info(obj)// { hello: 'world' }
        
    

### Settings

#### Member Visibility

*   Protected
*   Private
*   Inherited
*   External

#### Theme

OSLightDark

[get](#get)

Interface Helia
===============

The API presented by a Helia node

    [blockstore](helia.Helia.html#blockstore)

(): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<void\>;

<void\>;  
}

Properties
----------

### blockstore[](#blockstore)

blockstore: [Blocks](helia.Blocks.html)

Where the blocks are stored

### datastore[](#datastore)

datastore: [Datastore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html)
<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>

A key/value store

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

### dns[](#dns)

dns: [DNS](https://multiformats.github.io/js-dns/interfaces/index.DNS.html)

The DNS property can be used to perform lookups of various record types and will use a resolver appropriate to the current platform.

### getCodec[](#getCodec)

getCodec: [CodecLoader](helia.CodecLoader.html)

Load an IPLD codec. Implementations may return a promise if, for example, the codec is being fetched from the network.

### getHasher[](#getHasher)

getHasher: [HasherLoader](helia.HasherLoader.html)

Hashers can be used to hash a piece of data with the specified hashing algorithm. Implementations may return a promise if, for example, the hasher is being fetched from the network.

### logger[](#logger)

logger: [ComponentLogger](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.ComponentLogger.html)

A logging component that can be reused by consumers

### `Optional` metrics[](#metrics)

metrics?: [Metrics](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.Metrics.html)

A metrics object that can be used to collected arbitrary stats about node usage.

### pins[](#pins)

pins: [Pins](helia.Pins.html)

Pinning operations for blocks in the blockstore

### routing[](#routing)

routing: [Routing](helia.Routing.html)

The routing component allows performing operations such as looking up content providers, information about peers, etc.

Methods
-------

### gc[](#gc)

*   gc(options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>[](#gc.gc-1)
    
*   Remove any unpinned blocks from the blockstore
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional` options: [GCOptions](helia.GCOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)

### start[](#start)

*   start(): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>[](#start.start-1)
    
*   Starts the Helia node
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>
    

### stop[](#stop)

*   stop(): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>[](#stop.stop-1)
    
*   Stops the Helia node
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)

### `Optional` onProgress[](#onProgress)

onProgress?: ((evt) => void)

#### Type declaration

*   *   (evt): void
    *   #### Parameters
        
        *   evt: [ProgressEvents](helia.BlockRetrievalOptions.html#ProgressEvents)
            
        
        #### Returns void
        

### `Optional` signal[](#signal)

signal?: [AbortSignal](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/AbortSignal)

Methods
-------

### `Optional` validateFn[](#validateFn)

*   validateFn(block): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>[](#validateFn.validateFn-1)
    
*   A function that blockBrokers should call prior to returning a block to ensure it can maintain control of the block request flow. e.g. TrustedGatewayBlockBroker will use this to ensure that the block is valid from one of the gateways before assuming its work is done. If the block is not valid, it should try another gateway and WILL consider the gateway that returned the invalid blocks completely unreliable.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   block: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>
    

### Settings

#### Member Visibility

*   Protected
*   Private
*   Inherited
*   External

#### Theme

OSLightDark

### On This Page

[onProgress](#onProgress)
[signal](#signal)
[validateFn](#validateFn)

### createSession[](#createSession)

*   createSession(root, options?): [SessionBlockstore](helia.SessionBlockstore.html)
    [](#createSession.createSession-1)
    
*   #### Parameters
    
    *   root: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [CreateSessionOptions](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html)
        <[GetBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [SessionBlockstore](helia.SessionBlockstore.html)
    

### delete[](#delete)

*   delete(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <void\>[](#delete.delete-1)
    
*   Remove the record for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[DeleteBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.DeleteBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.delete(new Key('awesome'))console.log('deleted awesome content :(')
        
    

### deleteMany[](#deleteMany)

*   deleteMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#deleteMany.deleteMany-1)
    
*   Remove values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>\>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[DeleteManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.DeleteManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [new Key('awesome')]for await (const key of store.deleteMany(source)) {  console.log(`deleted content with key ${key}`)}
        
    

### get[](#get)

*   get(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>[](#get.get-1)
    
*   Retrieve the value stored under the given key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetOfflineOptions](helia.GetOfflineOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[GetBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const value = await store.get(new Key('awesome'))console.log('got content: %s', value.toString('utf8'))// => got content: datastore
        
    

### getAll[](#getAll)

*   getAll(options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>[](#getAll.getAll-1)
    
*   Retrieve all cid/block pairs from the blockstore as an unordered iterable
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[GetAllBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetAllBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { multihash, block } of store.getAll()) {  console.log('got:', multihash, block)  // => got MultihashDigest('Qmfoo') Uint8Array[...]}
        
    

### getMany[](#getMany)

*   getMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>[](#getMany.getMany-1)
    
*   Retrieve values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>\>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetOfflineOptions](helia.GetOfflineOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[GetManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { key, value } of store.getMany([new Key('awesome')])) {  console.log(`got "${key}" = "${new TextDecoder('utf8').decode(value)}"`')  // => got "/awesome" = "datastore"}
        
    

### has[](#has)

*   has(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <boolean\>[](#has.has-1)
    
*   Check for the existence of a value for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[HasBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.HasBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <boolean\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const exists = await store.has(new Key('awesome'))if (exists) { console.log('it is there')} else { console.log('it is not there')}
        
    

### put[](#put)

*   put(key, val, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#put.put-1)
    
*   Store the passed value under the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   val: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[PutBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.PutBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.put([{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }])
        
    

### putMany[](#putMany)

*   putMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#putMany.putMany-1)
    
*   Store the given key/value pairs
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[PutManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.PutManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }]for await (const { key, value } of store.putMany(source)) {  console.info(`put content for key ${key}`)}

Interface CreateSessionOptions<ProgressEvents>

interface CreateSessionOptions<[ProgressEvents](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#ProgressEvents)
\> {  
    [maxProviders](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#maxProviders)
?: number;  
    [minProviders](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#minProviders)
?: number;  
    [onProgress](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#onProgress)
?: ((evt) => void);

?: [AbortSignal](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/AbortSignal)
;  
}

#### Type Parameters

*   ProgressEvents extends [ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
    <any, any\> = [ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
    <any, any\>

#### Hierarchy

*   [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)

    <[ProgressEvents](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#ProgressEvents)
    \>
    *   CreateSessionOptions
        

##### Index

### Properties

[maxProviders?](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#maxProviders)
 [minProviders?](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#minProviders)
 [onProgress?](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#onProgress)
 [signal?](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#signal)

Properties
----------

### `Optional` maxProviders[](#maxProviders)

maxProviders?: number

The maximum number of providers for the root CID to be added to a session.

#### Default

    5
    

### `Optional` minProviders[](#minProviders)

minProviders?: number

The minimum number of providers for the root CID that are required for successful session creation.

The session will become usable once this many providers have been discovered, up to `maxProviders` providers will continue to be added.

#### Default

    1
    

### `Optional` onProgress[](#onProgress)

onProgress?: ((evt) => void)

#### Type declaration

*   *   (evt): void
    *   #### Parameters
        
        *   evt: [ProgressEvents](helia.CreateSessionOptions.html#ProgressEvents)
            
        
        #### Returns void
        

### `Optional` signal[](#signal)

signal?: [AbortSignal](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/AbortSignal)

### Settings

*   Protected
*   Private
*   Inherited
*   External

OSLightDark

[onProgress](#onProgress)
[signal](#signal)

Methods
-------

### delete[](#delete)

*   delete(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <void\>[](#delete.delete-1)
    
*   Remove the record for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [DeleteOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#DeleteOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.delete(new Key('awesome'))console.log('deleted awesome content :(')
        
    

### deleteMany[](#deleteMany)

*   deleteMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#deleteMany.deleteMany-1)
    
*   Remove values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>\>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [DeleteManyOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#DeleteManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [new Key('awesome')]for await (const key of store.deleteMany(source)) {  console.log(`deleted content with key ${key}`)}
        
    

### get[](#get)

*   get(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>[](#get.get-1)
    
*   Retrieve the value stored under the given key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#GetOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const value = await store.get(new Key('awesome'))console.log('got content: %s', value.toString('utf8'))// => got content: datastore
        
    

### getAll[](#getAll)

*   getAll(options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>[](#getAll.getAll-1)
    
*   Retrieve all cid/block pairs from the blockstore as an unordered iterable
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetAllOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#GetAllOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { multihash, block } of store.getAll()) {  console.log('got:', multihash, block)  // => got MultihashDigest('Qmfoo') Uint8Array[...]}
        
    

### getMany[](#getMany)

*   getMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>[](#getMany.getMany-1)
    
*   Retrieve values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>\>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetManyOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#GetManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { key, value } of store.getMany([new Key('awesome')])) {  console.log(`got "${key}" = "${new TextDecoder('utf8').decode(value)}"`')  // => got "/awesome" = "datastore"}
        
    

### has[](#has)

*   has(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <boolean\>[](#has.has-1)
    
*   Check for the existence of a value for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [HasOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#HasOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <boolean\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const exists = await store.has(new Key('awesome'))if (exists) { console.log('it is there')} else { console.log('it is not there')}
        
    

### put[](#put)

*   put(key, val, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#put.put-1)
    
*   Store the passed value under the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   val: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [PutOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#PutOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.put([{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }])
        
    

### putMany[](#putMany)

*   putMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#putMany.putMany-1)
    
*   Store the given key/value pairs
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Pair](interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [PutManyOptionsExtension](interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html#PutManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }]for await (const { key, value } of store.putMany(source)) {  console.info(`put content for key ${key}`)}
        
    

### Settings

*   Inherited

Interface HeliaInit<T>
======================

Options used to create a Helia node.

interface HeliaInit<[T](helia.HeliaInit.html#T)
\> {  
    [blockBrokers](helia.HeliaInit.html#blockBrokers)
: ((components) => [BlockBroker](helia.BlockBroker.html)
<[ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
<any, any\>, [ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
<any, any\>\>)\[\];  
    [blockstore](helia.HeliaInit.html#blockstore)
: [Blockstore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html)
<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>;  
    [codecs](helia.HeliaInit.html#codecs)
?: [BlockCodec](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/codecs_interface.BlockCodec.html)
<any, any\>\[\];  
    [components](helia.HeliaInit.html#components)
?: [Record](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#recordkeys-type)
<string, any\>;  
    [datastore](helia.HeliaInit.html#datastore)
: [Datastore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html)
<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>;  
    [dns](helia.HeliaInit.html#dns)
?: [DNS](https://multiformats.github.io/js-dns/interfaces/index.DNS.html)
;  
    [hashers](helia.HeliaInit.html#hashers)
?: [MultihashHasher](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/hashes_interface.MultihashHasher.html)
<number\>\[\];  
    [holdGcLock](helia.HeliaInit.html#holdGcLock)
?: boolean;  
    [keychain](helia.HeliaInit.html#keychain)
?: [KeychainInit](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_keychain.KeychainInit.html)
;  
    [libp2p](helia.HeliaInit.html#libp2p)
?: [T](helia.HeliaInit.html#T)
 | [Omit](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#omittype-keys)
<[Libp2pOptions](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/types/libp2p.index.Libp2pOptions.html)
<any\>, "start"\>;  
    [logger](helia.HeliaInit.html#logger)
?: [ComponentLogger](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.ComponentLogger.html)
;  
    [metrics](helia.HeliaInit.html#metrics)
?: [Metrics](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.Metrics.html)
;  
    [providerLookupConcurrency](helia.HeliaInit.html#providerLookupConcurrency)
?: number;  
    [routers](helia.HeliaInit.html#routers)
?: [Partial](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#partialtype)
<[Routing](helia.Routing.html)
\>\[\];  
    [start](helia.HeliaInit.html#start)
?: boolean;  
    [loadCodec](helia.HeliaInit.html#loadCodec.loadCodec-1)
?(code): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
<[BlockCodec](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/codecs_interface.BlockCodec.html)
<any, any\>\>;  
    [loadHasher](helia.HeliaInit.html#loadHasher.loadHasher-1)
?(code): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
<[MultihashHasher](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/hashes_interface.MultihashHasher.html)
<number\>\>;  
}

<[ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
<any, any\>, [ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
<any, any\>\>)\[\]

A list of strategies used to fetch blocks when they are not present in the local blockstore

#### Type declaration

*   *   (components): [BlockBroker](helia.BlockBroker.html)
        <[ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
        <any, any\>, [ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
        <any, any\>\>
    *   #### Parameters
        
        *   components: any
        
        #### Returns [BlockBroker](helia.BlockBroker.html)
        <[ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
        <any, any\>, [ProgressEvent](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressEvent.html)
        <any, any\>\>
        

### blockstore[](#blockstore)

blockstore: [Blockstore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html)
<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>

The blockstore is where blocks are stored

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

### `Optional` codecs[](#codecs)

codecs?: [BlockCodec](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/codecs_interface.BlockCodec.html)
<any, any\>\[\]

In order to pin CIDs that correspond to a DAG, it's necessary to know how to traverse that DAG. DAGWalkers take a block and yield any CIDs encoded within that block.

### `Optional` components[](#components)

components?: [Record](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#recordkeys-type)
<string, any\>

Components used by subclasses

### datastore[](#datastore)

datastore: [Datastore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html)
<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>

The datastore is where data is stored

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

### `Optional` dns[](#dns)

dns?: [DNS](https://multiformats.github.io/js-dns/interfaces/index.DNS.html)

An optional DNS implementation used to perform queries for DNS records.

### `Optional` hashers[](#hashers)

hashers?: [MultihashHasher](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/hashes_interface.MultihashHasher.html)
<number\>\[\]

By default sha256, sha512 and identity hashes are supported for bitswap operations. To bitswap blocks with CIDs using other hashes pass appropriate MultihashHashers here.

### `Optional` holdGcLock[](#holdGcLock)

holdGcLock?: boolean

Garbage collection requires preventing blockstore writes during searches for unpinned blocks as DAGs are typically pinned after they've been imported - without locking this could lead to the deletion of blocks while they are being added to the blockstore.

By default this lock is held on the current process and other processes will contact this process for access.

If Helia is being run in multiple processes, one process must hold the GC lock so use this option to control which process that is.

#### Default

    true
    

### `Optional` keychain[](#keychain)

keychain?: [KeychainInit](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_keychain.KeychainInit.html)

By default Helia stores the node's PeerId in an encrypted form in a libp2p keystore. These options control how that keystore is configured.

### `Optional` libp2p[](#libp2p)

libp2p?: [T](helia.HeliaInit.html#T)
 | [Omit](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#omittype-keys)
<[Libp2pOptions](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/types/libp2p.index.Libp2pOptions.html)
<any\>, "start"\>

A libp2p node is required to perform network operations. Either a pre-configured node or options to configure a node can be passed here.

If node options are passed, they will be merged with the default config for the current platform. In this case all passed config keys will replace those from the default config.

The libp2p `start` option is not supported, instead please pass `start` in the root of the HeliaInit object.

### `Optional` logger[](#logger)

logger?: [ComponentLogger](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.ComponentLogger.html)

An optional logging component to pass to libp2p. If not specified the default implementation from libp2p will be used.

### `Optional` metrics[](#metrics)

metrics?: [Metrics](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.Metrics.html)

A metrics object that can be used to collected arbitrary stats about node usage.

### `Optional` providerLookupConcurrency[](#providerLookupConcurrency)

providerLookupConcurrency?: number

During provider lookups, peers can be returned from routing implementations with no multiaddrs.

This can happen when they've been retrieved from network peers that only store multiaddrs for a limited amount of time.

When this happens the peer's info has to be looked up with a further query.

To not have this query block the yielding of other providers returned with multiaddrs, a separate queue is used to perform this lookup.

This config value controls the concurrency of that queue.

#### Default

    5
    

### `Optional` routers[](#routers)

routers?: [Partial](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#partialtype)
<[Routing](helia.Routing.html)
\>\[\]

Routers perform operations such as looking up content providers, information about network peers or getting/putting records.

### `Optional` start[](#start)

start?: boolean

Pass `false` to not start the Helia node

Methods
-------

*   loadCodec(code): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[BlockCodec](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/codecs_interface.BlockCodec.html)
    <any, any\>\>[](#loadCodec.loadCodec-1)
    
*   An optional function that can load a BlockCodec on demand. May return a promise.

    <[BlockCodec](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/codecs_interface.BlockCodec.html)
    <any, any\>\>
    

### `Optional` loadHasher[](#loadHasher)

    <[MultihashHasher](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/hashes_interface.MultihashHasher.html)
    <number\>\>[](#loadHasher.loadHasher-1)

    <[MultihashHasher](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/hashes_interface.MultihashHasher.html)
    <number\>\>

Where the blocks are stored

### datastore[](#datastore)

<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>

A key/value store

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

### dns[](#dns)

dns: [DNS](https://multiformats.github.io/js-dns/interfaces/index.DNS.html)

The DNS property can be used to perform lookups of various record types and will use a resolver appropriate to the current platform.

### getCodec[](#getCodec)

getCodec: [CodecLoader](helia.CodecLoader.html)

Load an IPLD codec. Implementations may return a promise if, for example, the codec is being fetched from the network.

### getHasher[](#getHasher)

getHasher: [HasherLoader](helia.HasherLoader.html)

Hashers can be used to hash a piece of data with the specified hashing algorithm. Implementations may return a promise if, for example, the hasher is being fetched from the network.

### libp2p[](#libp2p)

libp2p: [T](helia.HeliaLibp2p.html#T)

### logger[](#logger)

logger: [ComponentLogger](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.ComponentLogger.html)

A logging component that can be reused by consumers

### `Optional` metrics[](#metrics)

metrics?: [Metrics](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.Metrics.html)

A metrics object that can be used to collected arbitrary stats about node usage.

### pins[](#pins)

pins: [Pins](helia.Pins.html)

Pinning operations for blocks in the blockstore

### routing[](#routing)

routing: [Routing](helia.Routing.html)

The routing component allows performing operations such as looking up content providers, information about peers, etc.

(cid, options?): [AsyncGenerator](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/AsyncGenerator)
<[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
<unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
\>, void, undefined\>;  
    [setMetadata](helia.Pins.html#setMetadata.setMetadata-1)
(cid, metadata, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<void\>;  
}

##### Index

Methods
-------

### add[](#add)

*   add(cid, options?): [AsyncGenerator](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/AsyncGenerator)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>, void, undefined\>[](#add.add-1)
    
*   Pin a block in the blockstore. It will not be deleted when garbage collection is run.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   cid: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AddOptions](helia.AddOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [AsyncGenerator](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/AsyncGenerator)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>, void, undefined\>
    

### get[](#get)

*   get(cid, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[Pin](helia.Pin.html)
    \>[](#get.get-1)
    
*   If the CID is pinned, return details of the pin, otherwise throw an error
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   cid: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.AbortOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[Pin](helia.Pin.html)
    \>
    

### isPinned[](#isPinned)

*   isPinned(cid, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <boolean\>[](#isPinned.isPinned-1)
    
*   Return true if the passed CID is pinned
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   cid: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>

    <boolean\>

    <[Pin](helia.Pin.html)
    , void, undefined\>[](#ls.ls-1)
    
*   List all blocks that have been pinned.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional` options: [LsOptions](helia.LsOptions.html)

    <[Pin](helia.Pin.html)
    , void, undefined\>
    

### rm[](#rm)

    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>, void, undefined\>[](#rm.rm-1)

    \>, void, undefined\>
