# Provider | Helia

*   [Helia](../index.html)
    
*   [helia](../modules/helia.html)
    
*   [Provider](helia.Provider.html)
    

Interface Provider
==================

A provider can supply the content for a CID

interface Provider {  
    [id](helia.Provider.html#id)
: [PeerId](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/types/_libp2p_interface.PeerId.html)
;  
    [multiaddrs](helia.Provider.html#multiaddrs)
: [Multiaddr](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiaddr/interfaces/index.Multiaddr.html)
\[\];  
    [protocols](helia.Provider.html#protocols)
?: string\[\];  
}

#### Hierarchy

*   [PeerInfo](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.PeerInfo.html)
    *   Provider

##### Index

### Properties

[id](helia.Provider.html#id)
 [multiaddrs](helia.Provider.html#multiaddrs)
 [protocols?](helia.Provider.html#protocols)

Properties
----------

### id[](#id)

id: [PeerId](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/types/_libp2p_interface.PeerId.html)

The identifier of the remote peer

### multiaddrs[](#multiaddrs)

multiaddrs: [Multiaddr](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiaddr/interfaces/index.Multiaddr.html)
\[\]

The multiaddrs a peer is listening on

### `Optional` protocols[](#protocols)

protocols?: string\[\]

If present these are the methods that the peer can supply the content via.

If not present the caller should attempt to dial the remote peer and run the identify protocol to discover how to retrieve the content.

Example values are (but not limited to):

*   transport-graphsync-filecoinv1
*   transport-ipfs-gateway-http
*   transport-bitswap

### Settings

#### Member Visibility

*   Protected
*   Private
*   Inherited
*   External

#### Theme

OSLightDark

### On This Page

[id](#id)
[multiaddrs](#multiaddrs)
[protocols](#protocols)

Interface Routing
=================

interface Routing {  
    [cancelReprovide](helia.Routing.html#cancelReprovide.cancelReprovide-1)
(key, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<void\>;  
    [findPeer](helia.Routing.html#findPeer.findPeer-1)
(peerId, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[PeerInfo](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.PeerInfo.html)
\>;  
    [findProviders](helia.Routing.html#findProviders.findProviders-1)
(cid, options?): AsyncIterable<[Provider](helia.Provider.html)
\>;  
    [get](helia.Routing.html#get.get-1)
(key, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
\>;  
    [getClosestPeers](helia.Routing.html#getClosestPeers.getClosestPeers-1)
(key, options?): AsyncIterable<[PeerInfo](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.PeerInfo.html)
\>;  
    [provide](helia.Routing.html#provide.provide-1)
(cid, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<void\>;  
    [put](helia.Routing.html#put.put-1)
(key, value, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<void\>;  
}

##### Index

### Methods

[cancelReprovide](helia.Routing.html#cancelReprovide)
 [findPeer](helia.Routing.html#findPeer)
 [findProviders](helia.Routing.html#findProviders)
 [get](helia.Routing.html#get)

Methods
-------

### cancelReprovide[](#cancelReprovide)

*   cancelReprovide(key, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>[](#cancelReprovide.cancelReprovide-1)
    
*   Helia will periodically re-provide every previously provided CID. Use this method to no longer re-provide the passed CID.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.AbortOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        // ...await contentRouting.cancelReprovide(cid)
        
    

### findPeer[](#findPeer)

*   findPeer(peerId, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[PeerInfo](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.PeerInfo.html)
    \>[](#findPeer.findPeer-1)
    
*   Searches the network for peer info corresponding to the passed peer id.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   peerId: [PeerId](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/types/_libp2p_interface.PeerId.html)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [RoutingOptions](helia.RoutingOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[PeerInfo](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.PeerInfo.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        // ...const peer = await peerRouting.findPeer(peerId, options)
        
    

### findProviders[](#findProviders)

*   findProviders(cid, options?): AsyncIterable<[Provider](helia.Provider.html)
    \>[](#findProviders.findProviders-1)
    
*   Find the providers of the passed CID.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   cid: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [RoutingOptions](helia.RoutingOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns AsyncIterable<[Provider](helia.Provider.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        // Iterate over the providers found for the given cidfor await (const provider of contentRouting.findProviders(cid)) { console.log(provider.id, provider.multiaddrs)}
        
    

### get[](#get)

*   get(key, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>[](#get.get-1)
    
*   Retrieves a value from the network corresponding to the passed key.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [RoutingOptions](helia.RoutingOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        // ...const key = '/key'const value = await contentRouting.get(key)
        
    

### getClosestPeers[](#getClosestPeers)

*   getClosestPeers(key, options?): AsyncIterable<[PeerInfo](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.PeerInfo.html)
    \>[](#getClosestPeers.getClosestPeers-1)
    
*   Search the network for peers that are closer to the passed key. Peer info should be yielded in ever-increasing closeness to the key.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [RoutingOptions](helia.RoutingOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns AsyncIterable<[PeerInfo](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.PeerInfo.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        // Iterate over the closest peers found for the given keyfor await (const peer of peerRouting.getClosestPeers(key)) {  console.log(peer.id, peer.multiaddrs)}
        
    

### provide[](#provide)

*   provide(cid, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>[](#provide.provide-1)
    
*   The implementation of this method should ensure that network peers know the caller can provide content that corresponds to the passed CID.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   cid: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [RoutingOptions](helia.RoutingOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        // ...await contentRouting.provide(cid)
        
    

### put[](#put)

*   put(key, value, options?): [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>[](#put.put-1)
    
*   Puts a value corresponding to the passed key in a way that can later be retrieved by another network peer using the get method.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   value: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [RoutingOptions](helia.RoutingOptions.html)
        
    
    #### Returns [Promise](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        // ...const key = '/key'const value = uint8ArrayFromString('oh hello there')await contentRouting.put(key, value)
        
    

### Settings

#### Member Visibility

*   Protected
*   Private
*   Inherited
*   External

#### Theme

OSLightDark

# RoutingOptions | Helia

*   [Helia](../index.html)
    
*   [helia](../modules/helia.html)
    
*   [RoutingOptions](helia.RoutingOptions.html)
    

Interface RoutingOptions
========================

When a routing operation involves reading values, these options allow controlling where the values are read from. Some implementations support a local cache that may be used in preference over network calls, for example when a record has a TTL.

interface RoutingOptions {  
    [onProgress](helia.RoutingOptions.html#onProgress)
?: ((evt) => void);  
    [signal](helia.RoutingOptions.html#signal)
?: [AbortSignal](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/AbortSignal)
;  
    [trace](helia.RoutingOptions.html#trace)
?: any;  
    [useCache](helia.RoutingOptions.html#useCache)
?: boolean;  
    [useNetwork](helia.RoutingOptions.html#useNetwork)
?: boolean;  
    [validate](helia.RoutingOptions.html#validate)
?: boolean;  
}

#### Hierarchy

*   [AbortOptions](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.AbortOptions.html)
    
*   [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    
*   [TraceOptions](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.TraceOptions.html)
    *   RoutingOptions

##### Index

### Properties

[onProgress?](helia.RoutingOptions.html#onProgress)
 [signal?](helia.RoutingOptions.html#signal)
 [trace?](helia.RoutingOptions.html#trace)
 [useCache?](helia.RoutingOptions.html#useCache)
 [useNetwork?](helia.RoutingOptions.html#useNetwork)
 [validate?](helia.RoutingOptions.html#validate)

Properties
----------

### `Optional` onProgress[](#onProgress)

onProgress?: ((evt) => void)

#### Type declaration

*   *   (evt): void
    *   #### Parameters
        
        *   evt: any
        
        #### Returns void
        

### `Optional` signal[](#signal)

signal?: [AbortSignal](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/AbortSignal)

### `Optional` trace[](#trace)

trace?: any

### `Optional` useCache[](#useCache)

useCache?: boolean

Pass `false` to not use cached values

#### Default

    true
    

### `Optional` useNetwork[](#useNetwork)

useNetwork?: boolean

Pass `false` to not use the network

#### Default

    true
    

### `Optional` validate[](#validate)

validate?: boolean

Pass `false` to not perform validation

#### Default

    true

*   Private
*   Inherited

# SessionBlockstore | Helia

*   [Helia](../index.html)
    
*   [helia](../modules/helia.html)
    
*   [SessionBlockstore](helia.SessionBlockstore.html)
    

Interface SessionBlockstore
===========================

A session blockstore is a special blockstore that only pulls content from a subset of network peers which respond as having the block for the initial root CID.

Any blocks written to the blockstore as part of the session will propagate to the blockstore the session was created from.

interface SessionBlockstore {  
    [close](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#close.close-1)
(): void;  
    [delete](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#delete.delete-1)
(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
<void\>;  
    [deleteMany](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#deleteMany.deleteMany-1)
(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
<[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
<unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
\>\>;  
    [get](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#get.get-1)
(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
<[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
\>;  
    [getAll](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#getAll.getAll-1)
(options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
<[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
\>;  
    [getMany](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#getMany.getMany-1)
(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
<[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
\>;  
    [has](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#has.has-1)
(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
<boolean\>;  
    [put](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#put.put-1)
(key, val, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
<[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
<unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
\>\>;  
    [putMany](helia.SessionBlockstore.html#putMany.putMany-1)
(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
<[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
<unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
\>\>;  
}

#### Hierarchy

*   [Blockstore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html)
    <[ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[HasBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.HasBlockProgressEvents.html)
    \>, [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[PutBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.PutBlockProgressEvents.html)
    \>, [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[PutManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.PutManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
    \>, [GetOfflineOptions](helia.GetOfflineOptions.html)
     & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[GetBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetBlockProgressEvents.html)
    \>, [GetOfflineOptions](helia.GetOfflineOptions.html)
     & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[GetManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
    \>, [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[GetAllBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetAllBlocksProgressEvents.html)
    \>, [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[DeleteBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.DeleteBlockProgressEvents.html)
    \>, [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
    <[DeleteManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.DeleteManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
    \>\>
    *   SessionBlockstore

Methods
-------

### close[](#close)

*   close(): void[](#close.close-1)
    
*   Any in-progress operations will be aborted.
    
    #### Returns void
    

### delete[](#delete)

*   delete(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <void\>[](#delete.delete-1)
    
*   Remove the record for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[DeleteBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.DeleteBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.delete(new Key('awesome'))console.log('deleted awesome content :(')
        
    

### deleteMany[](#deleteMany)

*   deleteMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#deleteMany.deleteMany-1)
    
*   Remove values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>\>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[DeleteManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.DeleteManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [new Key('awesome')]for await (const key of store.deleteMany(source)) {  console.log(`deleted content with key ${key}`)}
        
    

### get[](#get)

*   get(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>[](#get.get-1)
    
*   Retrieve the value stored under the given key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetOfflineOptions](helia.GetOfflineOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[GetBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const value = await store.get(new Key('awesome'))console.log('got content: %s', value.toString('utf8'))// => got content: datastore
        
    

### getAll[](#getAll)

*   getAll(options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>[](#getAll.getAll-1)
    
*   Retrieve all cid/block pairs from the blockstore as an unordered iterable
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[GetAllBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetAllBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { multihash, block } of store.getAll()) {  console.log('got:', multihash, block)  // => got MultihashDigest('Qmfoo') Uint8Array[...]}
        
    

### getMany[](#getMany)

*   getMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>[](#getMany.getMany-1)
    
*   Retrieve values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>\>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetOfflineOptions](helia.GetOfflineOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[GetManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.GetManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { key, value } of store.getMany([new Key('awesome')])) {  console.log(`got "${key}" = "${new TextDecoder('utf8').decode(value)}"`')  // => got "/awesome" = "datastore"}
        
    

### has[](#has)

*   has(key, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <boolean\>[](#has.has-1)
    
*   Check for the existence of a value for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[HasBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.HasBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <boolean\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const exists = await store.has(new Key('awesome'))if (exists) { console.log('it is there')} else { console.log('it is not there')}
        
    

### put[](#put)

*   put(key, val, options?): [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#put.put-1)
    
*   Store the passed value under the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
        <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
        \>
    *   val: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[PutBlockProgressEvents](../types/helia.PutBlockProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/helia.Await.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.put([{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }])
        
    

### putMany[](#putMany)

*   putMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>[](#putMany.putMany-1)
    
*   Store the given key/value pairs
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Pair](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Pair.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [ProgressOptions](https://achingbrain.github.io/progress-events/interfaces/ProgressOptions.html)
        <[PutManyBlocksProgressEvents](../types/helia.PutManyBlocksProgressEvents.html)
        \>
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/helia.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[CID](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/classes/cid.CID.html)
    <unknown, number, number, [Version](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/types/link_interface.Version.html)
    \>\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }]for await (const { key, value } of store.putMany(source)) {  console.info(`put content for key ${key}`)}

Methods
-------

### batch[](#batch)

*   batch(): [Batch](interface_datastore.index.Batch.html)
    <[BatchOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#BatchOptionsExtension)
    \>[](#batch.batch-1)
    
*   This will return an object with which you can chain multiple operations together, with them only being executed on calling `commit`.
    
    #### Returns [Batch](interface_datastore.index.Batch.html)
    <[BatchOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#BatchOptionsExtension)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const b = store.batch()for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {  b.put(new Key(`hello${i}`), new TextEncoder('utf8').encode(`hello world ${i}`))}await b.commit()console.log('put 100 values')
        
    

### delete[](#delete)

*   delete(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <void\>[](#delete.delete-1)
    
*   Remove the record for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [DeleteOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#DeleteOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.delete(new Key('awesome'))console.log('deleted awesome content :(')

*   deleteMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    \>[](#deleteMany.deleteMany-1)

    \>

*   get(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)

### getMany[](#getMany)

*   getMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_datastore.index.Pair.html)
    \>[](#getMany.getMany-1)
    
*   Retrieve values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetManyOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#GetManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_datastore.index.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { key, value } of store.getMany([new Key('awesome')])) {  console.log(`got "${key}" = "${new TextDecoder('utf8').decode(value)}"`')  // => got "/awesome" = "datastore"}
        
    

### has[](#has)

*   has(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <boolean\>[](#has.has-1)
    
*   Check for the existence of a value for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [HasOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#HasOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <boolean\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const exists = await store.has(new Key('awesome'))if (exists) { console.log('it is there')} else { console.log('it is not there')}
        
    

### put[](#put)

*   put(key, val, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    \>[](#put.put-1)
    
*   Store the passed value under the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        
    *   val: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [PutOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#PutOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.put([{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }])
        
    

### putMany[](#putMany)

*   putMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    \>[](#putMany.putMany-1)
    
*   Store the given key/value pairs
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Pair](interface_datastore.index.Pair.html)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [PutManyOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#PutManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }]for await (const { key, value } of store.putMany(source)) {  console.info(`put content for key ${key}`)}
        
    

### query[](#query)

*   query(query, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_datastore.index.Pair.html)
    \>[](#query.query-1)
    
*   Query the datastore.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   query: [Query](interface_datastore.index.Query.html)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [QueryOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#QueryOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_datastore.index.Pair.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        // retrieve __all__ key/value pairs from the storelet list = []for await (const { key, value } of store.query({})) {  list.push(value)}console.log('ALL THE VALUES', list)
        
    

### queryKeys[](#queryKeys)

*   queryKeys(query, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    \>[](#queryKeys.queryKeys-1)
    
*   Query the datastore.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   query: [KeyQuery](interface_datastore.index.KeyQuery.html)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [QueryKeysOptionsExtension](interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html#QueryKeysOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](../classes/interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        // retrieve __all__ keys from the storelet list = []for await (const key of store.queryKeys({})) {  list.push(key)}console.log('ALL THE KEYS', key)
        
    

### Settings

#### Member Visibility

*   Protected
*   Private
*   Inherited
*   External

# IPFS Stores

IPFS Stores
-----------

[stores](#md:stores)

=====================

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/js-stores.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/js-stores)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/js-stores/js-test-and-release.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/actions/workflows/js-test-and-release.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

> Blockstores and datastores used by IP-JS internals

[Packages](#md:packages)

=========================

*   [`packages/blockstore-core`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-core)
     Contains various implementations of the API contract described in interface-blockstore
*   [`packages/blockstore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-fs)
     Blockstore implementation with file system backend
*   [`packages/blockstore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-idb)
     Blockstore implementation with IndexedDB backend
*   [`packages/blockstore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-level)
     Blockstore implementation with level(up|down) backend
*   [`packages/blockstore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-s3)
     IPFS blockstore implementation backed by s3
*   [`packages/datastore-core`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-core)
     Wrapper implementation for interface-datastore
*   [`packages/datastore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-fs)
     Datastore implementation with file system backend
*   [`packages/datastore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-idb)
     Datastore implementation with IndexedDB backend.
*   [`packages/datastore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-level)
     Datastore implementation with level(up|down) backend
*   [`packages/datastore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-s3)
     IPFS datastore implementation backed by s3
*   [`packages/interface-blockstore`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-blockstore)
     An interface for storing and retrieving blocks
*   [`packages/interface-blockstore-tests`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-blockstore-tests)
     Compliance tests for the blockstore interface
*   [`packages/interface-datastore`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-datastore)
     datastore interface
*   [`packages/interface-datastore-tests`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-datastore-tests)
     Compliance tests for the datastore interface
*   [`packages/interface-store`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-store)
     A generic interface for storing and retrieving data

[API Docs](#md:api-docs)

=========================

*   [https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores)
    

[License](#md:license)

=======================

Licensed under either of

*   Apache 2.0, ([LICENSE-APACHE](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/LICENSE-APACHE)
     / [http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
    )
*   MIT ([LICENSE-MIT](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/LICENSE-MIT)
     / [http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT](http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
    )

[Contribute](#md:contribute)

=============================

Contributions welcome! Please check out [the issues](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/issues)
.

Also see our [contributing document](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING_JS.md)
 for more information on how we work, and about contributing in general.

Please be aware that all interactions related to this repo are subject to the IPFS [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/code-of-conduct.md)
.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

# interface-blockstore | IPFS Stores

*   [IPFS Stores](../index.html)
    
*   [interface-blockstore](interface_blockstore.html)
    

Module interface-blockstore
===========================

A Blockstore is a key/value database that lets use CIDs to store/retrieve binary blobs.

It is used by IPFS to store/retrieve the block that a given CID resolves to.

[Implementations](#md:implementations)

---------------------------------------

*   File System: [`blockstore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-fs)
    
*   IndexedDB: [`blockstore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/blockstore-idb)
    
*   level: [`blockstore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-level)
     (supports any levelup compatible backend)
*   Memory: [`blockstore-core/memory`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/blockstore-core/src/memory.ts)
    
*   S3: [`blockstore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-s3)
    

[interface-blockstore](#md:interface-blockstore)

=================================================

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/js-stores.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/js-stores)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/js-stores/js-test-and-release.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/actions/workflows/js-test-and-release.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

> An interface for storing and retrieving blocks

[About](#md:about)

===================

A Blockstore is a key/value database that lets use CIDs to store/retrieve binary blobs.

It is used by IPFS to store/retrieve the block that a given CID resolves to.

[Implementations](#md:implementations)

---------------------------------------

*   File System: [`blockstore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-fs)
    
*   IndexedDB: [`blockstore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/blockstore-idb)
    
*   level: [`blockstore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-level)
     (supports any levelup compatible backend)
*   Memory: [`blockstore-core/memory`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/blockstore-core/src/memory.ts)
    
*   S3: [`blockstore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-s3)
    

[Install](#md:install)

=======================

    $ npm i interface-blockstore
    

[API Docs](#md:api-docs)

=========================

*   [https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/modules/interface\_blockstore.html](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/modules/interface_blockstore.html)

### blockstore[](#blockstore)

blockstore: [Blockstore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_blockstore.Blockstore.html)
<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>

The blockstore is where blocks are stored

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

### `Optional` codecs[](#codecs)

codecs?: [BlockCodec](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/codecs_interface.BlockCodec.html)
<any, any\>\[\]

In order to pin CIDs that correspond to a DAG, it's necessary to know how to traverse that DAG. DAGWalkers take a block and yield any CIDs encoded within that block.

### `Optional` components[](#components)

components?: [Record](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#recordkeys-type)
<string, any\>

Components used by subclasses

### datastore[](#datastore)

datastore: [Datastore](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/interfaces/interface_datastore.index.Datastore.html)
<{}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}, {}\>

The datastore is where data is stored

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

#### Type declaration

### `Optional` dns[](#dns)

dns?: [DNS](https://multiformats.github.io/js-dns/interfaces/index.DNS.html)

An optional DNS implementation used to perform queries for DNS records.

### `Optional` hashers[](#hashers)

hashers?: [MultihashHasher](https://multiformats.github.io/js-multiformats/interfaces/hashes_interface.MultihashHasher.html)
<number\>\[\]

By default sha256, sha512 and identity hashes are supported for bitswap operations. To bitswap blocks with CIDs using other hashes pass appropriate MultihashHashers here.

### `Optional` holdGcLock[](#holdGcLock)

holdGcLock?: boolean

Garbage collection requires preventing blockstore writes during searches for unpinned blocks as DAGs are typically pinned after they've been imported - without locking this could lead to the deletion of blocks while they are being added to the blockstore.

By default this lock is held on the current process and other processes will contact this process for access.

If Helia is being run in multiple processes, one process must hold the GC lock so use this option to control which process that is.

#### Default

    true
    

### `Optional` logger[](#logger)

logger?: [ComponentLogger](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.ComponentLogger.html)

An optional logging component to pass to libp2p. If not specified the default implementation from libp2p will be used.

### `Optional` metrics[](#metrics)

metrics?: [Metrics](https://libp2p.github.io/js-libp2p/interfaces/_libp2p_interface.Metrics.html)

A metrics object that can be used to collected arbitrary stats about node usage.

### `Optional` providerLookupConcurrency[](#providerLookupConcurrency)

providerLookupConcurrency?: number

During provider lookups, peers can be returned from routing implementations with no multiaddrs.

This can happen when they've been retrieved from network peers that only store multiaddrs for a limited amount of time.

When this happens the peer's info has to be looked up with a further query.

To not have this query block the yielding of other providers returned with multiaddrs, a separate queue is used to perform this lookup.

This config value controls the concurrency of that queue.

#### Default

    5
    

### `Optional` routers[](#routers)

routers?: [Partial](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#partialtype)
<[Routing](helia.Routing.html)
\>\[\]

Routers perform operations such as looking up content providers, information about network peers or getting/putting records.

======================================================================================================================================================================================================

interface Store<[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
, [Value](interface_store.Store.html#Value)
, [Pair](interface_store.Store.html#Pair)
, [HasOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#HasOptionsExtension)
, [PutOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#PutOptionsExtension)
, [PutManyOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#PutManyOptionsExtension)
, [GetOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#GetOptionsExtension)
, [GetManyOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#GetManyOptionsExtension)
, [DeleteOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#DeleteOptionsExtension)
, [DeleteManyOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#DeleteManyOptionsExtension)
\> {  
    [delete](interface_store.Store.html#delete.delete-1)
(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
<void\>;  
    [deleteMany](interface_store.Store.html#deleteMany.deleteMany-1)
(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
<[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
\>;  
    [get](interface_store.Store.html#get.get-1)
(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
<[Value](interface_store.Store.html#Value)
\>;  
    [getMany](interface_store.Store.html#getMany.getMany-1)
(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
<[Pair](interface_store.Store.html#Pair)
\>;  
    [has](interface_store.Store.html#has.has-1)
(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
<boolean\>;  
    [put](interface_store.Store.html#put.put-1)
(key, val, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
<[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
\>;  
    [putMany](interface_store.Store.html#putMany.putMany-1)
(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
<[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
\>;  
}

*   Store
    

##### Index

### Methods

[delete](interface_store.Store.html#delete)
 [deleteMany](interface_store.Store.html#deleteMany)

 [getMany](interface_store.Store.html#getMany)

Methods
-------

### delete[](#delete)

*   delete(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <void\>[](#delete.delete-1)
    
*   Remove the record for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [DeleteOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#DeleteOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <void\>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.delete(new Key('awesome'))console.log('deleted awesome content :(')
        
    

### deleteMany[](#deleteMany)

*   deleteMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
    \>[](#deleteMany.deleteMany-1)
    
*   Remove values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [DeleteManyOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#DeleteManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [new Key('awesome')]for await (const key of store.deleteMany(source)) {  console.log(`deleted content with key ${key}`)}
        
    

### get[](#get)

*   get(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Value](interface_store.Store.html#Value)
    \>[](#get.get-1)
    
*   Retrieve the value stored under the given key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#GetOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Value](interface_store.Store.html#Value)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const value = await store.get(new Key('awesome'))console.log('got content: %s', value.toString('utf8'))// => got content: datastore
        
    

### getMany[](#getMany)

*   getMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_store.Store.html#Pair)
    \>[](#getMany.getMany-1)
    
*   Retrieve values for the passed keys
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [GetManyOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#GetManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Pair](interface_store.Store.html#Pair)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        for await (const { key, value } of store.getMany([new Key('awesome')])) {  console.log(`got "${key}" = "${new TextDecoder('utf8').decode(value)}"`')  // => got "/awesome" = "datastore"}
        
    

### has[](#has)

*   has(key, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <boolean\>[](#has.has-1)
    
*   Check for the existence of a value for the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [HasOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#HasOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <boolean\>
    
    #### Example
    
        const exists = await store.has(new Key('awesome'))if (exists) { console.log('it is there')} else { console.log('it is not there')}
        
    

### put[](#put)

*   put(key, val, options?): [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
    \>[](#put.put-1)
    
*   Store the passed value under the passed key
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
        
    *   val: [Value](interface_store.Store.html#Value)
        
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [PutOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#PutOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [Await](../types/interface_store.Await.html)
    <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        await store.put([{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }])
        
    

### putMany[](#putMany)

*   putMany(source, options?): [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
    \>[](#putMany.putMany-1)
    
*   Store the given key/value pairs
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   source: [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
        <[Pair](interface_store.Store.html#Pair)
        \>
    *   `Optional` options: [AbortOptions](interface_store.AbortOptions.html)
         & [PutManyOptionsExtension](interface_store.Store.html#PutManyOptionsExtension)
        
    
    #### Returns [AwaitIterable](../types/interface_store.AwaitIterable.html)
    <[Key](interface_store.Store.html#Key)
    \>
    
    #### Example
    
        const source = [{ key: new Key('awesome'), value: new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]) }]for await (const { key, value } of store.putMany(source)) {  console.info(`put content for key ${key}`)}

# interface-datastore | IPFS Stores

*   [IPFS Stores](../index.html)
    
*   [interface-datastore](interface_datastore.html)
    

Module interface-datastore
==========================

[interface-datastore](#md:interface-datastore)

===============================================

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/js-stores.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/js-stores)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/js-stores/js-test-and-release.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/actions/workflows/js-test-and-release.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

> datastore interface

[About](#md:about)

===================

A Datastore is a key/value database that lets store/retrieve binary blobs using namespaced Keys.

It is used by IPFS to store/retrieve arbitrary metadata needed to run the node - DHT provider records, signed peer records, etc.

[Backed Implementations](#md:backed-implementations)

-----------------------------------------------------

*   File System: [`datastore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-fs)
    
*   IndexedDB: [`datastore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-idb)
    
*   level: [`datastore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-level)
     (supports any levelup compatible backend)
*   Memory: [`datastore-core/memory`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/memory.ts)
    
*   S3: [`datastore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-s3)
    

[Wrapper Implementations](#md:wrapper-implementations)

-------------------------------------------------------

*   Keytransform: [`datstore-core/src/keytransform`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/keytransform.ts)
    
*   Mount: [`datastore-core/src/mount`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/mount.ts)
    
*   Namespace: [`datastore-core/src/namespace`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/namespace.ts)
    
*   Sharding: [`datastore-core/src/sharding`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/sharding.ts)
    
*   Tiered: [`datstore-core/src/tiered`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/tiered.ts)
    

If you want the same functionality as [go-ds-flatfs](https://github.com/ipfs/go-ds-flatfs)
, use sharding with fs.

[Example](#md:example)

-----------------------

    import FsStore from 'datastore-fs'import { ShardingDataStore, shard } from 'datastore-core'const fs = new FsStore('path/to/store')// flatfs now works like go-flatfsconst flatfs = await ShardingStore.createOrOpen(fs, new shard.NextToLast(2))
    

### [Test suite](#md:test-suite)

Available via the [`interface-datastore-tests`](https://npmjs.com/package/interface-datastore-tests)
 module

    import { interfaceDatastoreTests } from 'interface-datastore-tests'describe('mystore', () => {  interfaceDatastoreTests({    async setup () {      return instanceOfMyStore    },    async teardown () {      // cleanup resources    }  })})
    

### [Aborting requests](#md:aborting-requests)

Most API methods accept an \[AbortSignal\]\[\] as part of an options object. Implementations may listen for an `abort` event emitted by this object, or test the `signal.aborted` property. When received implementations should tear down any long-lived requests or resources created.

### [Concurrency](#md:concurrency)

The streaming `(put|get|delete)Many` methods are intended to be used with modules such as [it-parallel-batch](https://www.npmjs.com/package/it-parallel-batch)
 to allow calling code to control levels of parallelisation. The batching method ensures results are returned in the correct order, but interface implementations should be thread safe.

    import batch from 'it-parallel-batch'const source = [{  key: ..,  value: ..}]// put values into the datastore concurrently, max 10 at a timefor await (const { key, data } of batch(store.putMany(source), 10)) {  console.info(`Put ${key}`)}
    

### [Keys](#md:keys)

To allow a better abstraction on how to address values, there is a `Key` class which is used as identifier. It's easy to create a key from a `Uint8Array` or a `string`.

    const a = new Key('a')const b = new Key(new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]))
    

The key scheme is inspired by file systems and Google App Engine key model. Keys are meant to be unique across a system. They are typically hierarchical, incorporating more and more specific namespaces. Thus keys can be deemed 'children' or 'ancestors' of other keys:

*   `new Key('/Comedy')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython')`

Also, every namespace can be parameterized to embed relevant object information. For example, the Key `name` (most specific namespace) could include the object type:

*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Sketch:CheeseShop')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Sketch:CheeseShop/Character:Mousebender')`

[Install](#md:install)

=======================

    $ npm i interface-datastore
    

[Browser `<script>` tag](#md:browser-ltscriptgt-tag)

-----------------------------------------------------

Loading this module through a script tag will make its exports available as `InterfaceDatastore` in the global namespace.

    <script src="https://unpkg.com/interface-datastore/dist/index.min.js"></script>
    

[API Docs](#md:api-docs)

=========================

*   [https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/modules/interface\_datastore.html](https://ipfs.github.io/js-stores/modules/interface_datastore.html)
    

[License](#md:license)

=======================

Licensed under either of

*   Apache 2.0, ([LICENSE-APACHE](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/interface-datastore/LICENSE-APACHE)
     / [http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
    )
*   MIT ([LICENSE-MIT](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/interface-datastore/LICENSE-MIT)
     / [http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT](http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
    )

[Contribute](#md:contribute)

=============================

Contributions welcome! Please check out [the issues](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/issues)
.

Also see our [contributing document](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING_JS.md)
 for more information on how we work, and about contributing in general.

Please be aware that all interactions related to this repo are subject to the IPFS [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/code-of-conduct.md)
.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

[![](https://cdn.rawgit.com/jbenet/contribute-ipfs-gif/master/img/contribute.gif)](https://github.com/ipfs/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

# index | IPFS Stores

*   [IPFS Stores](../index.html)
    
*   [interface-datastore](interface_datastore.html)
    
*   [index](interface_datastore.index.html)
    

Module index
============

A Datastore is a key/value database that lets store/retrieve binary blobs using namespaced Keys.

It is used by IPFS to store/retrieve arbitrary metadata needed to run the node - DHT provider records, signed peer records, etc.

[Backed Implementations](#md:backed-implementations)

-----------------------------------------------------

*   File System: [`datastore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-fs)
    
*   IndexedDB: [`datastore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-idb)
    
*   level: [`datastore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-level)
     (supports any levelup compatible backend)
*   Memory: [`datastore-core/memory`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/memory.ts)
    
*   S3: [`datastore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-s3)
    

[Wrapper Implementations](#md:wrapper-implementations)

-------------------------------------------------------

*   Keytransform: [`datstore-core/src/keytransform`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/keytransform.ts)
    
*   Mount: [`datastore-core/src/mount`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/mount.ts)
    
*   Namespace: [`datastore-core/src/namespace`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/namespace.ts)
    
*   Sharding: [`datastore-core/src/sharding`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/sharding.ts)
    
*   Tiered: [`datstore-core/src/tiered`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/blob/main/packages/datastore-core/src/tiered.ts)
    

If you want the same functionality as [go-ds-flatfs](https://github.com/ipfs/go-ds-flatfs)
, use sharding with fs.

#### Example

    import FsStore from 'datastore-fs'import { ShardingDataStore, shard } from 'datastore-core'const fs = new FsStore('path/to/store')// flatfs now works like go-flatfsconst flatfs = await ShardingStore.createOrOpen(fs, new shard.NextToLast(2))
    

### [Test suite](#md:test-suite)

Available via the [`interface-datastore-tests`](https://npmjs.com/package/interface-datastore-tests)
 module

    import { interfaceDatastoreTests } from 'interface-datastore-tests'describe('mystore', () => {  interfaceDatastoreTests({    async setup () {      return instanceOfMyStore    },    async teardown () {      // cleanup resources    }  })})
    

### [Aborting requests](#md:aborting-requests)

Most API methods accept an \[AbortSignal\]\[\] as part of an options object. Implementations may listen for an `abort` event emitted by this object, or test the `signal.aborted` property. When received implementations should tear down any long-lived requests or resources created.

### [Concurrency](#md:concurrency)

The streaming `(put|get|delete)Many` methods are intended to be used with modules such as [it-parallel-batch](https://www.npmjs.com/package/it-parallel-batch)
 to allow calling code to control levels of parallelisation. The batching method ensures results are returned in the correct order, but interface implementations should be thread safe.

    import batch from 'it-parallel-batch'const source = [{  key: ..,  value: ..}]// put values into the datastore concurrently, max 10 at a timefor await (const { key, data } of batch(store.putMany(source), 10)) {  console.info(`Put ${key}`)}
    

### [Keys](#md:keys)

To allow a better abstraction on how to address values, there is a `Key` class which is used as identifier. It's easy to create a key from a `Uint8Array` or a `string`.

    const a = new Key('a')const b = new Key(new Uint8Array([0, 1, 2, 3]))
    

The key scheme is inspired by file systems and Google App Engine key model. Keys are meant to be unique across a system. They are typically hierarchical, incorporating more and more specific namespaces. Thus keys can be deemed 'children' or 'ancestors' of other keys:

*   `new Key('/Comedy')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython')`

Also, every namespace can be parameterized to embed relevant object information. For example, the Key `name` (most specific namespace) could include the object type:

*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Sketch:CheeseShop')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Sketch:CheeseShop/Character:Mousebender')`

Module interface-store
======================

An abstraction of the Datastore/Blockstore codebases.

[interface-store](#md:interface-store)

=======================================

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)

 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/js-stores/js-test-and-release.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/actions/workflows/js-test-and-release.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

> A generic interface for storing and retrieving data

[About](#md:about)

===================

An abstraction of the Datastore/Blockstore codebases.

# IPFS Stores

IPFS Stores
-----------

[stores](#md:stores)

=====================

[![ipfs.tech](https://img.shields.io/badge/project-IPFS-blue.svg?style=flat-square)](https://ipfs.tech)
 [![Discuss](https://img.shields.io/discourse/https/discuss.ipfs.tech/posts.svg?style=flat-square)](https://discuss.ipfs.tech)
 [![codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/ipfs/js-stores.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/ipfs/js-stores)
 [![CI](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/ipfs/js-stores/js-test-and-release.yml?branch=main&style=flat-square)](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/actions/workflows/js-test-and-release.yml?query=branch%3Amain)

> Blockstores and datastores used by IP-JS internals

[Packages](#md:packages)

=========================

*   [`packages/blockstore-core`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-core)
     Contains various implementations of the API contract described in interface-blockstore
*   [`packages/blockstore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-fs)
     Blockstore implementation with file system backend
*   [`packages/blockstore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-idb)
     Blockstore implementation with IndexedDB backend
*   [`packages/blockstore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-level)
     Blockstore implementation with level(up|down) backend
*   [`packages/blockstore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/blockstore-s3)
     IPFS blockstore implementation backed by s3
*   [`packages/datastore-core`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-core)
     Wrapper implementation for interface-datastore
*   [`packages/datastore-fs`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-fs)
     Datastore implementation with file system backend
*   [`packages/datastore-idb`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-idb)
     Datastore implementation with IndexedDB backend.
*   [`packages/datastore-level`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-level)
     Datastore implementation with level(up|down) backend
*   [`packages/datastore-s3`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/datastore-s3)
     IPFS datastore implementation backed by s3
*   [`packages/interface-blockstore`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-blockstore)
     An interface for storing and retrieving blocks
*   [`packages/interface-blockstore-tests`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-blockstore-tests)
     Compliance tests for the blockstore interface
*   [`packages/interface-datastore`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-datastore)
     datastore interface
*   [`packages/interface-datastore-tests`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-datastore-tests)
     Compliance tests for the datastore interface
*   [`packages/interface-store`](https://github.com/ipfs/js-stores/tree/main/packages/interface-store)
     A generic interface for storing and retrieving data

# Key | IPFS Stores

*   [IPFS Stores](../index.html)
    
*   [interface-datastore](../modules/interface_datastore.html)
    
*   [key](../modules/interface_datastore.key.html)
    
*   [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    

Class Key
=========

A Key represents the unique identifier of an object. Our Key scheme is inspired by file systems and Google App Engine key model. Keys are meant to be unique across a system. Keys are hierarchical, incorporating more and more specific namespaces. Thus keys can be deemed 'children' or 'ancestors' of other keys:

*   `new Key('/Comedy')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython')` Also, every namespace can be parametrized to embed relevant object information. For example, the Key `name` (most specific namespace) could include the object type:
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Sketch:CheeseShop')`
*   `new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Sketch:CheeseShop/Character:Mousebender')`

##### Index

### Constructors

[constructor](interface_datastore.key.Key.html#constructor)

### Properties

[\_buf](interface_datastore.key.Key.html#_buf)

 [clean](interface_datastore.key.Key.html#clean)

 [instance](interface_datastore.key.Key.html#instance)
 [isAncestorOf](interface_datastore.key.Key.html#isAncestorOf)
 [isDecendantOf](interface_datastore.key.Key.html#isDecendantOf)
 [isTopLevel](interface_datastore.key.Key.html#isTopLevel)

Constructors
------------

### constructor[](#constructor)

*   new Key(s, clean?): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#constructor.new_Key)
    
*   #### Parameters
    
    *   s: string | [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
        
    *   `Optional` clean: boolean
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    

Properties
----------

### `Private` \_buf[](#_buf)

\_buf: [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)

Accessors
---------

### \[toStringTag\][](#_toStringTag_)

*   get \[toStringTag\](): string
*   Return string representation of the key
    
    #### Returns string
    

Methods
-------

### baseNamespace[](#baseNamespace)

*   baseNamespace(): string[](#baseNamespace.baseNamespace-1)
    
*   Returns the "base" namespace of this key.
    
    #### Returns string
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese').baseNamespace()// => 'Actor:JohnCleese'
        
    

### child[](#child)

*   child(key): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#child.child-1)
    
*   Returns the `child` Key of this Key.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        
        The child Key to add
        
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython').child(new Key('Actor:JohnCleese'))// => Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese')
        
    

### clean[](#clean)

*   clean(): void[](#clean.clean-1)
    
*   Cleanup the current key
    
    #### Returns void
    

### concat[](#concat)

*   concat(...keys): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#concat.concat-1)
    
*   Concats one or more Keys into one new Key.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Rest` ...keys: [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        \[\]
        
        The array of keys to concatenate
        
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    

### instance[](#instance)

*   instance(s): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#instance.instance-1)
    
*   Returns an "instance" of this type key (appends value to namespace).
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   s: string
        
        The string to append.
        
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor').instance('JohnClesse')// => Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese')
        
    

### isAncestorOf[](#isAncestorOf)

*   isAncestorOf(other): boolean[](#isAncestorOf.isAncestorOf-1)
    
*   Returns whether this key is a prefix of `other`
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   other: [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        
        The other key to test against
        
    
    #### Returns boolean
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy').isAncestorOf('/Comedy/MontyPython')// => true
        
    

### isDecendantOf[](#isDecendantOf)

*   isDecendantOf(other): boolean[](#isDecendantOf.isDecendantOf-1)
    
*   Returns whether this key is a contains another as prefix.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   other: [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        
        The other Key to test against
        
    
    #### Returns boolean
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython').isDecendantOf('/Comedy')// => true
        
    

### isTopLevel[](#isTopLevel)

*   isTopLevel(): boolean[](#isTopLevel.isTopLevel-1)
    
*   Checks if this key has only one namespace.
    
    #### Returns boolean
    

### less[](#less)

*   less(key): boolean[](#less.less-1)
    
*   Check if the given key is sorted lower than ourself.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   key: [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
        
        The other Key to check against
        
    
    #### Returns boolean
    

### list[](#list)

*   list(): string\[\][](#list.list-1)
    
*   Returns the `list` representation of this key.
    
    #### Returns string\[\]
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese').list()// => ['Comedy', 'MontyPythong', 'Actor:JohnCleese']
        
    

### name[](#name)

*   name(): string[](#name.name-1)
    
*   Returns the "name" of this key (field of last namespace).
    
    #### Returns string
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese').name()// => 'JohnCleese'
        
    

### namespaces[](#namespaces)

*   namespaces(): string\[\][](#namespaces.namespaces-1)
    
*   Returns the `namespaces` making up this Key.
    
    #### Returns string\[\]
    

### parent[](#parent)

*   parent(): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#parent.parent-1)
    
*   Returns the `parent` Key of this Key.
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key("/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese").parent()// => Key("/Comedy/MontyPython")
        
    

### path[](#path)

*   path(): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#path.path-1)
    
*   Returns the "path" of this key (parent + type).
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese').path()// => Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor')
        
    

### reverse[](#reverse)

*   reverse(): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#reverse.reverse-1)
    
*   Returns the key with all parts in reversed order.
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese').reverse()// => Key('/Actor:JohnCleese/MontyPython/Comedy')
        
    

### toString[](#toString)

*   toString(encoding?): string[](#toString.toString-1)
    
*   Convert to the string representation
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   `Optional` encoding: [SupportedEncodings](https://achingbrain.github.io/uint8arrays/types/index.SupportedEncodings.html)
         = 'utf8'
        
        The encoding to use.
        
    
    #### Returns string
    

### type[](#type)

*   type(): string[](#type.type-1)
    
*   Returns the "type" of this key (value of last namespace).
    
    #### Returns string
    
    #### Example
    
        new Key('/Comedy/MontyPython/Actor:JohnCleese').type()// => 'Actor'
        
    

### uint8Array[](#uint8Array)

*   uint8Array(): [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    [](#uint8Array.uint8Array-1)
    
*   Return the Uint8Array representation of the key
    
    #### Returns [Uint8Array](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
    

### `Static` asKey[](#asKey)

*   asKey(other): null | [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#asKey.asKey-1)
    
*   #### Parameters
    
    *   other: any
    
    #### Returns null | [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    

### `Static` random[](#random)

*   random(): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#random.random-1)
    
*   Returns a randomly (uuid) generated key.
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    
    #### Example
    
        Key.random()// => Key('/344502982398')
        
    

### `Static` withNamespaces[](#withNamespaces)

*   withNamespaces(list): [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    [](#withNamespaces.withNamespaces-1)
    
*   Constructs a key out of a namespace array.
    
    #### Parameters
    
    *   list: string\[\]
        
        The array of namespaces
        
    
    #### Returns [Key](interface_datastore.key.Key.html)
    
    #### Example
    
        Key.withNamespaces(['one', 'two'])// => Key('/one/two')
